Empires & Puzzles Pertinax Hero Review: The Knight Guardian of Owl Tower

Is Pertinax the Owl Tower's savior or a niche trickster? We breakdown his unique 'Owlhalla' ghost mechanic, analyze his stats against the modern 2026 meta, and decide if this Holy Paladin is worth your Ascension Mats. Read the full review.
Pertinax Review: The Owl of Vengeance or Just a Ghost Story?
Pertinax: The Owl That Turns You Into A Ghost (Literally)

Pertinax, the "Knight Guardian of Owl Tower," swoops into Empires & Puzzles as a 5-star Holy Paladin with one of the most text-heavy special skills we've seen in a while. Released in early 2025 as part of the Owl Tower family, he brings the signature "Moonrise Strike" mechanic and a unique vengeance gimmick that punishes enemies for popping his minions. While he lacks a costume, his base form is designed to be a disruptor, turning attackers into useless ghosts. He's a bird with a grudge, and if you touch his friends, you're going to the shadow realm (or Owlhalla, as he calls it).
Visually, Pertinax is a standout. He's not just an owl; he's a

owl. Clad in golden armor with a stoic, "I've seen things" expression, he perches majestically on a sword hilt. The art team nailed the "Guardian" vibe—he looks like he would tirelessly guard a library or a sacred grove, pecking the eyes out of anyone who talks too loud. The color palette of rich browns, golds, and the glowing shield creates a warm yet imposing presence. It's a design that says, "I'm wise, but I will also mess you up."
When we look at Pertinax's stats in the context of the current early 2026 meta, the "aging" process is visible.
Compare this to modern heavy hitters like

(1308 Atk) who are pushing 1300+ in primary stats and 2250+ in HP. Pertinax is sitting about 100-150 points behind the cutting edge. While not obsolete—his Paladin class and decent bulk help—he relies heavily on his mechanics rather than raw stat-checking opponents. He won't survive simply by existing; he needs his minions and passives to stay relevant.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Owl family bonus is a niche but powerful counter-measure. Replacing mana ailments with mana generation buffs turns a weakness into a strength. Against mana-slowing tanks (like the classic Waterpipe or modern Slime heroes), Pertinax actually speeds up.
passive is arguably his best feature. Reducing mana gain from Special Skills by
enemies. This is a hard check on the "Special Skill Mana" meta. Heroes that rely on giving mana chunks to allies (Xnolphod style effects) are essentially neutered just by Pertinax being on the field.

is a solid, if unexciting, Aether power. Since he is a Paladin and wants to be hit to trigger his class talent (and his minions want to be hit to trigger the trap), reflecting some damage back fits his "thorns" playstyle.

Upon release, Pertinax was viewed as a "high text, high complexity" hero. The Owl Tower event itself brought mechanics that players found either fascinating or tedious. Pertinax stood out as a punisher of the "Minion destroyer" meta.
His stats have fallen off. The 1161 Attack stat is low for a damage dealer in late 2025/early 2026. However, his
(Moonrise Strike) help him bypass the insane Defense stats of modern tanks.

He is not a "plug and play" hero for every raid. In top-tier play, the prevalence of "Toon" passives (75% chance to resist ailments) hurts him bad. If a Toon kills his minion and resists the Ghost form, Pertinax loses a huge chunk of his value. However, in
or against specific mana-heavy teams, his passive alone makes him viable.

to cast his special, summon minions, and keep his passive active. His damage output is partially fixed (Ghost DOT) or capped (Moonrise), so raw Attack is less critical than staying power.

The current meta is dominated by heroes who resist ailments (Toons, Monks, Clerics). If the Ghost effect is resisted, Pertinax does very little.
On offense, you can choose not to kill his minions. He relies on the opponent making a mistake or being forced to use AoE.
He is squishier than the newest Construct or Astral gods.
His mechanics are convoluted. If you don't understand how "Full Removal" interacts with specific immunities, you might waste his potential.
His mana speed becomes Very Fast, and his passive cripples enemy mana boosters.
Minions provide "fake healing" (HP buffer), and the Ghost damage racks up without healers to mitigate it.

The community has a love-hate relationship with Pertinax.

Pertinax is a prime example of a hero who looks like a god on paper but struggles against the specific immunities of his era. He is the

. When his trap works—stripping a fully buffed god-hero naked and turning them into a helpless ghost—it is the most satisfying feeling in Empires & Puzzles. When it gets resisted by a pesky Toon passive, he feels like just another average-speed hitter. Use him for his passive, enjoy the minions, and pray to the RNG gods for the Ghost form to land.
A strong technical hero for your roster depth, but not the meta-breaker the prophecies foretold.
Currently, Pertinax does not have a costume available, which leaves him reliant on his base stats and mechanics to compete.
A comprehensive review of Pertinax, the 5-star Holy Paladin from the Owl Tower event. Analysis of his Vengeance of Owlhalla skill, stats, emblem paths, and modern viability in Empires & Puzzles.
